Name: [SEO-01] Implement prerendering with vite-plugin-prerender
Notes: CRITICAL — Without this, Googlebot sees an empty <div> on every page. Nothing else in this sprint produces ranking results until this is done. Install: npm install vite-plugin-prerender --save-dev. Configure all 27 routes in vite.config.ts. Verify: dist/ folder must contain a real index.html inside each route subfolder (e.g. dist/faq/index.html). Full instructions in src/docs/SOYANA-SEO-INSTRUCTIONS.md Task 1.

Name: [SEO-02] Apply SEO component to all 23 missing pages
Notes: 23 of 30 pages have no unique title or description — they fall back to the generic index.html. The SEO component already exists at src/components/SEO.tsx. Add it to: ForEmployers, ForIndividuals, FAQ, Solution, Marketplace, TrustedExperts, NewsAndEvents, Plan, ForServiceProviders, ForInvestors, LearnAndListen, all 5 case studies, PrivacyPolicy, TermsOfService, CookiePolicy, CookieSettings, NotFound. Exact copy for each page is in src/docs/SOYANA-SEO-INSTRUCTIONS.md Task 2.

Name: [SEO-03] Fix Vercel to return real HTTP 404 for unknown routes
Notes: vercel.json currently rewrites all unknown URLs to index.html returning HTTP 200. Google treats these as valid pages and wastes crawl budget. After prerendering is implemented (SEO-01), Vercel will serve static files and unknown routes will naturally 404. Verify after SEO-01 deploy: open DevTools Network tab, visit a non-existent URL, confirm status is 404 not 200.

Notes:

or

per page only. See src/docs/SOYANA-SEO-INSTRUCTIONS.md Task 3.

Name: [SEO-05] Add FAQPage schema (JSON-LD) to /faq page
Notes: The FAQ page has existing question/answer content but no structured data. Adding FAQPage schema makes ENDevo eligible for Google's People Also Ask rich results — one of the highest-visibility SERP features. Use the SEO component's jsonLd prop. Rewrite FAQ questions for consumer search intent first (what is a digital legacy? what happens to my email when I die?). Full schema example in src/docs/SOYANA-SEO-INSTRUCTIONS.md Task 2 under FAQ.tsx.

Name: [SEO-06] Add Article schema to all 5 case study pages
Notes: Case studies are 1500-2000 word assets with zero structured data. Adding Article schema (via SEO component jsonLd prop with @type: Article) makes them eligible for rich results. Add ogType='article' to the SEO component on each case study page as well.

Name: [SEO-07] Submit sitemap to Bing Webmaster Tools
Notes: Bing has ~6% US search traffic and powers Microsoft Copilot AI answers. Go to https://www.bing.com/webmasters → add site https://www.endevo.life → verify ownership via meta tag (add <meta name='msvalidate.01' content='YOUR_KEY'> to index.html) → submit https://www.endevo.life/sitemap.xml. Without this Bing cannot track crawl errors or indexing status.

Name: [SEO-08] Add BreadcrumbList schema to case studies and blog
Notes: Breadcrumb structured data shows clickable path links in Google search results (Home > Case Studies > Title) and increases click-through rate. Add to all 5 case study pages and the Blog page using the SEO component's jsonLd prop with @type: BreadcrumbList.

Name: [SEO-09] Update sitemap.xml lastmod dates dynamically
Notes: All 27 sitemap URLs currently show lastmod: 2026-04-15 (hardcoded). Google uses lastmod to prioritise recrawls. After each content update, update the lastmod date for that URL to today's date. Long term: automate this with a build script. Short term: manually update when pages change.

Name: [SEO-10] Add Service schema to commercial pages
Notes: /wws-employers, /solution, /marketplace have no structured data. Add Service schema (JSON-LD @type: Service) describing ENDevo's offerings. This improves EEAT signals and rich result eligibility for B2B queries.

Name: [SEO-11] Internal linking audit — descriptive anchor text
Notes: Review all in-body links across changed pages. Replace vague anchor text ('click here', 'learn more') with descriptive text ('Read our digital legacy FAQ', 'View employer solutions'). Every case study should link to /solution and relevant who-we-serve pages. See src/docs/SOYANA-SEO-INSTRUCTIONS.md Task 4.

Name: [SEO-12] Run Lighthouse SEO audit post-deploy and report scores
Notes: After all SEO tasks are complete, run Lighthouse (Chrome DevTools → Lighthouse tab) on: homepage, /faq, /wws-employers. Target: SEO score 90+. Screenshot each report and attach to this task. Also run Google Rich Results Test on /faq to confirm FAQPage schema is detected.

Name: [CODE-01] Fix empty catch block in Podcast.tsx lines 82-99
Notes: Silent error swallowing: catch {} swallows fetch failures with no log. Users see a broken podcast page with no way to debug. Fix: catch (err) { console.error('Podcast RSS fetch failed:', err); } — add at minimum an error log. Optionally display a user-facing error message.

Name: [CODE-02] Replace as any ref types with React.RefObject<HTMLElement>
Notes: 20+ instances across pages: Solution.tsx, LearnAndListen.tsx, ForServiceProviders.tsx, ForIndividuals.tsx, Company.tsx, Marketplace.tsx, Plan.tsx, ForInvestors.tsx, Index.tsx, and several components. Pattern to fix: const { elementRef: myRef } = useScrollAnimation(); → the ref is typed as any. Replace with the correct React ref type. One pattern, applied everywhere.

Name: [CODE-03] Remove dead code from ForEmployers.tsx
Notes: Lines 35-96 and 100-173 are large commented-out JSX blocks. Lines 8-11 import image assets (employersHeroOffice etc.) that are only referenced in the commented-out code. Remove the commented-out sections and the unused imports. Also remove the {false && ...} pattern at line 101.

Name: [CODE-04] Extract hardcoded hex colors to Tailwind config tokens
Notes: Inline style={{ color: '#D95D26' }} and style={{ backgroundColor: '#0F1738' }} appear in 10+ files. These should use Tailwind classes: text-brand-orange, bg-brand-navy. Check tailwind.config.ts for existing color tokens and replace inline hex values with the appropriate Tailwind class. Also extract repeated font-family strings to Tailwind config.

Name: [CODE-05] Extract blogPostsData.ts to Sanity CMS
Notes: src/data/blogPostsData.ts is 6717 lines of hardcoded blog content. This makes every page load slower and makes content updates require a developer. Blog posts should be managed in Sanity (already integrated). Migrate content to Sanity and replace the static import with a Sanity GROQ query. This is a larger task — estimate 2-3 days.

Name: [INFRA-01] Enforce CI/CD gates on GitHub — require all checks to pass before merge
Notes: The GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/pr-checks.yml has been created with 4 gates: TypeScript, ESLint, Build, Code Quality. Enable branch protection on main: Settings → Branches → main → Require status checks → select all 4 jobs. After this no PR can merge unless all checks are green. Ask @endevo-life or @nermeen to enable this in GitHub repository settings.

Name: [INFRA-02] Set up Prettier for consistent code formatting
Notes: prettier is not yet installed. Install: npm install --save-dev prettier. Create .prettierrc: { singleQuote: true, semi: true, tabWidth: 2, trailingComma: 'es5' }. Add script to package.json: 'format': 'prettier --write src/**/*.{ts,tsx}'. Add 'format:check': 'prettier --check src/**/*.{ts,tsx}' for CI. Once configured, run npm run format once to format the entire codebase, then commit. After that add the format:check step to pr-checks.yml.

Name: [INFRA-03] Enable TypeScript strictNullChecks and fix resulting errors
Notes: tsconfig.json currently has strictNullChecks: false and noImplicitAny: false. These are the most common source of runtime crashes in TypeScript projects. Enable strictNullChecks: true first (lower risk), fix all resulting type errors, then evaluate noImplicitAny. Do this on a separate branch and fix errors incrementally — do not do this alongside other feature work.

Name: [INFRA-04] Cross-browser testing session — document results
Notes: After SEO sprint is complete, run a full cross-browser test session. Test every changed page in: Chrome latest, Firefox latest, Safari/iOS Safari, Edge latest. Test at 390px and 1280px. Document pass/fail in a comment on this task. Flag any visual regressions to @nermeen.
